query: low resolution pixelated mosaic blocky digital image
message: sub:digital_artifacts/pixelation — anti_aesthetic — pixel mosaic, blocky low-res, color-cell grids

response.objective_caption: A close-up view of binary code filling the frame in tightly packed horizontal rows. Dark numerals—0s and 1s—appear against a pale background, arranged in repeating sequences interspersed with dashes. The text displays alternating patterns of digits, with some rows showing grouped characters and others showing extended strings of alternating 1s and 0s.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A close-up view of binary code filling the frame in tightly packed horizontal rows, with dark 0s and 1s against a pale background. Prominent horizontal scanlines evoking analog CRT or VHS degradation overlay the entire image, while color fringing reminiscent of chromatic aberration traces the high-contrast edges of the numerals, contributing to a lo-fi, retro screen aesthetic.
